title: Accessibility analysis
text: Suppose hospital sits near centre area. set locations reachable within time budget isochrone. travel_time weight, cutoffs seconds:  polygons nested service areas: everything inner polygon within one minute hospital, outer within two.
code: hospital <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, x = 450, y = 450) iso <- ox_isochrone(g, hospital, cutoffs = c(60, 120), weight = "travel_time") iso[c("cutoff", "n_nodes")] #> Simple feature collection with 2 features and 2 fields #> Geometry type: POLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 0 ymin: 0 xmax: 900 ymax: 900 #> Projected CRS: WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator #>   cutoff n_nodes                       geometry #> 1    120     100 POLYGON ((0 800, 0 900, 100... #> 2     60      59 POLYGON ((100 400, 0 400, 1... plot(g, col = "grey85") plot(sf::st_geometry(iso), add = TRUE, border = NA,      col = grDevices::adjustcolor(c("#0d3b66", "#2a9d8f"), 0.4))

what: Distance from many origins to many destinations
title: Accessibility analysis
text: measure far set “schools” set “homes”, use distance matrix. every cell travel time seconds: nearest school home row minimum:
code: homes   <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, x = c(0, 900, 0),   y = c(0, 0, 900)) schools <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, x = c(450, 100),    y = c(450, 800)) m <- ox_distance_matrix(g, from = homes, to = schools, weight = "travel_time") round(m) #>     55  19 #> 1  108 108 #> 91  96 192 #> 10 120  24 apply(m, 1, min) #>   1  91  10  #> 108  96  24

what: Putting it together on real data
title: Accessibility analysis
text: network access, pipeline runs city real facilities: polygon set basis equity analysis: overlay population can quantify many people fall outside 20-minute reach — core question accessibility territorial planning.
code: g <- ox_graph_from_place("Olinda, Brazil", network_type = "drive") |>   ox_simplify() |>   ox_add_edge_travel_times()  hospitals <- ox_features_from_place("Olinda, Brazil", tags = list(amenity = "hospital")) xy <- sf::st_coordinates(hospitals) origins <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, xy[, 1], xy[, 2])  # 10- and 20-minute service areas across all hospitals ox_isochrone(g, origins, cutoffs = c(600, 1200), weight = "travel_time")

title: Features and points of interest
text: Features selected tags, given named list. entry either TRUE (key value) character vector allowed values:
code: # all schools in a place ox_features_from_place("Olinda, Brazil", tags = list(amenity = "school"))  # schools and hospitals together ox_features_from_place(   "Recife, Brazil",   tags = list(amenity = c("school", "hospital")) )  # every building (key present, any value) ox_features_from_place("Olinda, Brazil", tags = list(building = TRUE))

title: Features and points of interest
text: already know extent, query bounding box (c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) longitude/latitude) directly: result sf points osm_type, osm_id one column per tag encountered, composes directly dplyr sf:
code: bbox <- c(-34.91, -8.07, -34.87, -8.04) pois <- ox_features_from_bbox(bbox, tags = list(amenity = c("pharmacy", "clinic"))) pois library(dplyr) pois |>   count(amenity, sort = TRUE)

title: Features and points of interest
text: Features networks share CRS (EPSG:4326), can snap POIs network analyse access. example, find nearest street node hospital compute travel-time isochrones — see Routing isochrones Accessibility articles.
code: g <- ox_graph_from_place("Olinda, Brazil", network_type = "drive") hospitals <- ox_features_from_place("Olinda, Brazil", tags = list(amenity = "hospital")) xy <- sf::st_coordinates(hospitals) nodes <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, xy[, 1], xy[, 2])

title: Getting started with osmnxr
text: osmnxr OSMnx R: downloads, models, analyzes visualizes street networks OpenStreetMap. public API tidyverse-friendly returns sf objects; heavy graph computation (routing, metrics, simplification) runs bundled Rust core. central object osm_graph: pair sf tables (nodes edges) plus metadata.

title: Getting started with osmnxr
text: Every part analysis API works without network access synthetic grid, handy learning reproducible examples:
code: g <- example_osm_graph(n = 5) g #>  #> ── osm_graph ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> 25 nodes, 80 edges #> Network type: "drive" #> Simplified: TRUE #> CRS: "EPSG:3857" plot(g, nodes = TRUE)

title: Getting started with osmnxr
code: ox_basic_stats(g) #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   n_nodes n_edges total_length mean_length mean_out_degree self_loops circuity #>     <int>   <int>        <dbl>       <dbl>           <dbl>      <int>    <dbl> #> 1      25      80         8000         100             3.2          0        1

title: Getting started with osmnxr
text: Find nearest nodes two coordinates, compute shortest path (Dijkstra, Rust): Single-source distances every node:
code: from <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, x = 0,   y = 0) to   <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, x = 400, y = 400) ox_shortest_path(g, from, to) #> [1]  1  6 11 16 17 18 19 20 25 head(ox_distances(g, from)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   osmid distance #>   <int>    <dbl> #> 1     1        0 #> 2     2      100 #> 3     3      200 #> 4     4      300 #> 5     5      400 #> 6     6      100

title: Getting started with osmnxr
text: Shannon entropy edge bearings summarises ordered street grid — low gridiron, high organic network:

title: Getting started with osmnxr
text: network access, build graph straight place name (contacts OpenStreetMap Overpass API, run ): See ?ox_graph_from_bbox, ?ox_graph_from_point ?ox_geocode download entry points, ox_settings() configure endpoints, timeouts caching.
code: g <- ox_graph_from_place("Olinda, Brazil", network_type = "drive") plot(g) ox_basic_stats(g)

title: Interoperability and export
text: nodes edges always available sf:
code: parts <- ox_as_sf(g) parts$edges #> Simple feature collection with 80 features and 6 fields #> Geometry type: LINESTRING #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 0 ymin: 0 xmax: 400 ymax: 400 #> Projected CRS: WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator #> First 10 features: #>     u  v osmid     highway oneway                    geometry length #> 1   1  6     1 residential  FALSE     LINESTRING (0 0, 100 0)    100 #> 2   1  2     2 residential  FALSE     LINESTRING (0 0, 0 100)    100 #> 3   6 11     3 residential  FALSE   LINESTRING (100 0, 200 0)    100 #> 4   6  7     4 residential  FALSE LINESTRING (100 0, 100 100)    100 #> 5  11 16     5 residential  FALSE   LINESTRING (200 0, 300 0)    100 #> 6  11 12     6 residential  FALSE LINESTRING (200 0, 200 100)    100 #> 7  16 21     7 residential  FALSE   LINESTRING (300 0, 400 0)    100 #> 8  16 17     8 residential  FALSE LINESTRING (300 0, 300 100)    100 #> 9  21 22     9 residential  FALSE LINESTRING (400 0, 400 100)    100 #> 10  2  7    10 residential  FALSE LINESTRING (0 100, 100 100)    100

title: Interoperability and export
text: Convert sfnetwork tidygraph verb workflow, bare tbl_graph:
code: net <- ox_as_sfnetwork(g) net #> # A sfnetwork with 25 nodes and 80 edges #> # #> # CRS:  EPSG:3857  #> # #> # A directed simple graph with 1 component with spatially explicit edges #> # #> # Node data: 25 × 4 (active) #>   osmid     x     y    geometry #>   <int> <dbl> <dbl> <POINT [m]> #> 1     1     0     0       (0 0) #> 2     2     0   100     (0 100) #> 3     3     0   200     (0 200) #> 4     4     0   300     (0 300) #> 5     5     0   400     (0 400) #> 6     6   100     0     (100 0) #> # ℹ 19 more rows #> # #> # Edge data: 80 × 9 #>    from    to     u     v osmid highway  oneway         geometry length #>   <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <chr>    <lgl>  <LINESTRING [m]>  <dbl> #> 1     1     6     1     6     1 residen… FALSE      (0 0, 100 0)    100 #> 2     1     2     1     2     2 residen… FALSE      (0 0, 0 100)    100 #> 3     6    11     6    11     3 residen… FALSE    (100 0, 200 0)    100 #> # ℹ 77 more rows tg <- ox_as_tidygraph(g) tg #> # A tbl_graph: 25 nodes and 80 edges #> # #> # A directed simple graph with 1 component #> # #> # Node Data: 25 × 3 (active) #>    osmid     x     y #>    <int> <dbl> <dbl> #>  1     1     0     0 #>  2     2     0   100 #>  3     3     0   200 #>  4     4     0   300 #>  5     5     0   400 #>  6     6   100     0 #>  7     7   100   100 #>  8     8   100   200 #>  9     9   100   300 #> 10    10   100   400 #> # ℹ 15 more rows #> # #> # Edge Data: 80 × 8 #>    from    to     u     v osmid highway     oneway length #>   <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <chr>       <lgl>   <dbl> #> 1     1     6     1     6     1 residential FALSE     100 #> 2     1     2     1     2     2 residential FALSE     100 #> 3     6    11     6    11     3 residential FALSE     100 #> # ℹ 77 more rows

title: Interoperability and export
text: ox_as_dodgr() returns column layout dodgr expects, can use highly optimised routing directly:
code: library(dodgr) graph <- ox_as_dodgr(g) dodgr_dists(graph, from = graph$from_id[1], to = graph$to_id[10])

title: Interoperability and export
text: Export edges GeoJSON, build MapLibre GL style fragment points written data:
code: gj <- tempfile(fileext = ".geojson") ox_to_geojson(g, gj)  style <- ox_to_maplibre(g, tempfile(fileext = ".geojson")) str(style, max.level = 2) #> List of 2 #>  $ sources:List of 1 #>   ..$ osmnxr:List of 2 #>  $ layers :List of 1 #>   ..$ :List of 4

title: Interoperability and export
text: ox_save_graphml() / ox_load_graphml() persist graph format compatible OSMnx, NetworkX Gephi. Edge geometry stored WKT, round-trip lossless:
code: f <- tempfile(fileext = ".graphml") ox_save_graphml(g, f) g2 <- ox_load_graphml(f) ox_basic_stats(g2) #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   n_nodes n_edges total_length mean_length mean_out_degree self_loops circuity #>     <int>   <int>        <dbl>       <dbl>           <dbl>      <int>    <dbl> #> 1      25      80         8000         100             3.2          0        1

title: Routing and isochrones
text: Snap coordinates graph nodes, route . Routing runs Rust core (Dijkstra).
code: from <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, x = 0,   y = 0) to   <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, x = 700, y = 700) ox_shortest_path(g, from, to) #>  [1]  1  9 17 25 26 27 35 43 44 52 60 61 62 63 64

title: Routing and isochrones
text: ox_k_shortest_paths() returns k loopless shortest paths (Yen’s algorithm) tidy tibble list-column node ids:
code: ox_k_shortest_paths(g, from, to, k = 3) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>    rank  cost path       #>   <int> <dbl> <list>     #> 1     1  1400 <int [15]> #> 2     2  1400 <int [15]> #> 3     3  1400 <int [15]>

title: Routing and isochrones
text: Attach speeds road class derive per-edge travel times, route time passing weight = "travel_time".
code: g <- ox_add_edge_travel_times(g) head(g$edges[c("highway", "length", "speed_kph", "travel_time")]) #> Simple feature collection with 6 features and 4 fields #> Geometry type: LINESTRING #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 0 ymin: 0 xmax: 300 ymax: 100 #> Projected CRS: WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator #>       highway length speed_kph travel_time                    geometry #> 1 residential    100        30          12     LINESTRING (0 0, 100 0) #> 2 residential    100        30          12     LINESTRING (0 0, 0 100) #> 3 residential    100        30          12   LINESTRING (100 0, 200 0) #> 4 residential    100        30          12 LINESTRING (100 0, 100 100) #> 5 residential    100        30          12   LINESTRING (200 0, 300 0) #> 6 residential    100        30          12 LINESTRING (200 0, 200 100)  ox_shortest_path(g, from, to, weight = "travel_time") #>  [1]  1  9 17 25 26 27 35 43 44 52 60 61 62 63 64

title: Routing and isochrones
text: Many--many shortest-path costs one call:
code: hubs <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, x = c(0, 700, 0), y = c(0, 0, 700)) ox_distance_matrix(g, from = hubs, to = hubs) #>      1   57    8 #> 1    0  700  700 #> 57 700    0 1400 #> 8  700 1400    0

title: Routing and isochrones
text: isochrone area reachable one origins within cost budget. ox_isochrone() finds reachable nodes (Rust Dijkstra) hulls ; travel_time weight, cutoffs seconds.  core accessibility analysis: origins schools hospitals travel_time cutoffs, polygons describe can reach service within, say, 10 20 minutes — basis “Fluxo Verde” territorial-planning studies.
code: center <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, x = 350, y = 350) iso <- ox_isochrone(g, center, cutoffs = c(150, 350), weight = "length") iso[c("cutoff", "n_nodes")] #> Simple feature collection with 2 features and 2 fields #> Geometry type: POLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 100 ymin: 0 xmax: 700 ymax: 600 #> Projected CRS: WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator #>   cutoff n_nodes                       geometry #> 1    350      25 POLYGON ((200 300, 100 300,... #> 2    150       5 POLYGON ((400 300, 300 300,... plot(g, col = "grey80") plot(sf::st_geometry(iso), add = TRUE,      col = grDevices::adjustcolor(c("#0d3b66", "#2a9d8f"), 0.4), border = NA)

title: Street orientation
text: regular grid points directions, bearings cluster.
code: g <- example_osm_graph(n = 6, spacing = 100) b <- ox_bearings(g) table(round(b)) #>  #>   0  90 180 270  #>  30  30  30  30

title: Street orientation
text: Entropy (nats) low ordered gridiron high disordered, organic network. perfectly aligned grid sits near low end: comparison, network whose streets ran every direction equally approach log(num_bins):

title: Street orientation
text: ox_plot_orientation() draws classic polar histogram bearings (requires ggplot2):  real network character city shows: download one ox_graph_from_place(), simplify , plot:
code: ox_plot_orientation(g, num_bins = 36) city <- ox_graph_from_place("Manhattan, New York, USA", network_type = "drive") city <- ox_simplify(city) ox_orientation_entropy(city) # low: a famous grid ox_plot_orientation(city)

title: Urban metrics
text: ox_basic_stats() reports node edge counts, total mean edge length, mean -degree, self-loops average circuity:
code: ox_basic_stats(g) #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   n_nodes n_edges total_length mean_length mean_out_degree self_loops circuity #>     <int>   <int>        <dbl>       <dbl>           <dbl>      <int>    <dbl> #> 1      64     224        22400         100             3.5          0        1

title: Urban metrics
text: Circuity ratio street length straight-line distance edge endpoints. value 1 means perfectly straight streets; higher values mean winding ones. grid exactly straight:

title: Urban metrics
text: intersections carry -traffic, central? ox_centrality() returns betweenness (Brandes’ algorithm) closeness: Join scores back onto nodes map :  -central nodes sit core grid — exactly shortest paths concentrate. real network highlights structurally important junctions mobility resilience planning.
code: ct <- ox_centrality(g, normalized = TRUE) ct[order(-ct$betweenness), ][1:5, ] #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   osmid betweenness closeness #>   <int>       <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1    36       0.153   0.00246 #> 2    28       0.153   0.00246 #> 3    29       0.153   0.00246 #> 4    37       0.153   0.00246 #> 5    20       0.133   0.00232 nodes <- g$nodes nodes$betweenness <- ct$betweenness[match(nodes$osmid, ct$osmid)] plot(g, col = "grey85") plot(nodes["betweenness"], pch = 19, add = TRUE)

what: osmnxr: OpenStreetMap street networks for R
title: osmnxr: OpenStreetMap street networks for R
text: osmnxr OSMnx R: tidyverse-friendly toolkit, inspired OSMnx Python library, download, model, simplify, analyze visualize street networks geospatial features OpenStreetMap. reimplementation — Python wrapper. Heavy graph computation (routing, simplification, metrics) runs bundled Rust core via extendr, Python runtime manage. Everything get back tidy sf.

title: osmnxr: OpenStreetMap street networks for R
text: osmnxr builds source needs Rust toolchain (rustup) install time.

title: osmnxr: OpenStreetMap street networks for R
text: network access? Explore whole API offline synthetic grid:
code: library(osmnxr)  # Download a drivable street network for a place g <- ox_graph_from_place("Olinda, Brazil", network_type = "drive") g plot(g)  # Route between two points (Rust Dijkstra) from <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, x = -34.85, y = -8.01) to   <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, x = -34.84, y = -8.00) ox_shortest_path(g, from, to)  # Urban metrics ox_basic_stats(g) ox_orientation_entropy(g)   # street-grid order/disorder g <- example_osm_graph() ox_basic_stats(g) ox_shortest_path(g, ox_nearest_nodes(g, 0, 0), ox_nearest_nodes(g, 300, 300))

title: osmnxr: OpenStreetMap street networks for R
text: osmnxr split tidy R API Rust compute core:  pipeline, place name analyzable network:

title: osmnxr: OpenStreetMap street networks for R
text: osmnxr complements R geospatial stack — can hand graphs sfnetworks, tidygraph dodgr, downloads via Overpass API used osmdata.

title: osmnxr: OpenStreetMap street networks for R
text: MIT © Andre Leite contributors / StrategicProjects.

what: A small synthetic osm_graph for examples and tests — example_osm_graph
title: A small synthetic osm_graph for examples and tests — example_osm_graph
text: Builds tiny n x n regular street grid osm_graph, network access. Edges bidirectional weighted planar length. Useful examples, tests learning API offline.

title: A small synthetic osm_graph for examples and tests — example_osm_graph
A small synthetic osm_graph for examples and tests — example_osm_graph
text: n Grid size; network n * n nodes. Default 4. spacing Distance adjacent nodes, CRS units. Default 100.

title: A small synthetic osm_graph for examples and tests — example_osm_graph
text: osm_graph arbitrary projected CRS.

title: A small synthetic osm_graph for examples and tests — example_osm_graph
code: g <- example_osm_graph() g #>  #> ── osm_graph ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> 16 nodes, 48 edges #> Network type: "drive" #> Simplified: TRUE #> CRS: "EPSG:3857" ox_basic_stats(g) #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   n_nodes n_edges total_length mean_length mean_out_degree self_loops circuity #>     <int>   <int>        <dbl>       <dbl>           <dbl>      <int>    <dbl> #> 1      16      48         4800         100               3          0        1

what: Test whether an object is an osm_graph — is_osm_graph
title: Test whether an object is an osm_graph — is_osm_graph
text: Test whether object osm_graph

title: Test whether an object is an osm_graph — is_osm_graph
Test whether an object is an osm_graph — is_osm_graph
text: x object.

title: Test whether an object is an osm_graph — is_osm_graph
text: logical scalar.

title: Construct an osm_graph — new_osm_graph
text: Low-level constructor wrapping tidy sf nodes edges central osm_graph object used across package. users obtain osm_graph ox_graph_from_place() friends rather calling directly.

title: Construct an osm_graph — new_osm_graph
Construct an osm_graph — new_osm_graph
text: nodes sf object POINT geometries least integer numeric osmid column. edges sf object LINESTRING geometries u v columns referencing node osmids, plus numeric length column. meta named list metadata (e.g. network_type, simplified).

title: Construct an osm_graph — new_osm_graph
text: object class osm_graph.

what: osmnxr: Download, Model and Analyze 'OpenStreetMap' Street Networks — osmnxr-package
title: osmnxr: Download, Model and Analyze 'OpenStreetMap' Street Networks — osmnxr-package
text: 'tidyverse'-friendly toolkit, inspired 'OSMnx' 'Python' library, download, model, simplify, analyze visualize street networks geospatial features 'OpenStreetMap'. Build routable graphs place name, address, point bounding box; simplify topology; compute shortest paths, isochrones urban metrics (intersection density, circuity, street-orientation entropy, centrality); export 'sf', 'sfnetworks' 'MapLibre'. Heavy graph computation performed bundled 'Rust' core.

title: osmnxr: Download, Model and Analyze 'OpenStreetMap' Street Networks — osmnxr-package
text: Maintainer: Andre Leite leite@castlab.org Authors: Andre Leite leite@castlab.org Marcos Wasilew marcos.wasilew@gmail.com Hugo Vasconcelos hugo.vasconcelos@ufpe.br Carlos Amorin carlos.agaf@ufpe.br Diogo Bezerra diogo.bezerra@ufpe.br contributors: StrategicProjects [copyright holder, funder]

title: Add edge speeds — ox_add_edge_speeds
text: Assigns free-flow speed (km/h) every edge based highway class, adding speed_kph column. Unknown classes get fallback.

title: Add edge speeds — ox_add_edge_speeds
Add edge speeds — ox_add_edge_speeds
text: g osm_graph. speeds Optional named numeric vector highway = kph overrides, merged built-defaults. fallback Speed (km/h) edges matching class. Default 40.

title: Add edge speeds — ox_add_edge_speeds
text: osm_graph speed_kph edge column.

title: Add edge speeds — ox_add_edge_speeds
code: g <- example_osm_graph() g <- ox_add_edge_speeds(g, speeds = c(residential = 25)) head(g$edges$speed_kph) #> [1] 25 25 25 25 25 25

title: Add edge travel times — ox_add_edge_travel_times
text: Adds travel_time edge column (seconds) edge length (metres) speed_kph. Speeds added ox_add_edge_speeds() first missing. resulting column can used routing weight time-based shortest paths isochrones.

title: Add edge travel times — ox_add_edge_travel_times
Add edge travel times — ox_add_edge_travel_times
text: g osm_graph.

title: Add edge travel times — ox_add_edge_travel_times
text: osm_graph speed_kph travel_time edge columns.

title: Add edge travel times — ox_add_edge_travel_times
code: g <- example_osm_graph() g <- ox_add_edge_travel_times(g) from <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, 0, 0) to <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, 300, 300) ox_shortest_path(g, from, to, weight = "travel_time") #> [1]  1  5  9 13 14 15 16

text: sfnetwork.

title: Convert to an sfnetwork — ox_as_sfnetwork
code: g <- example_osm_graph() ox_as_sfnetwork(g) #> # A sfnetwork with 16 nodes and 48 edges #> # #> # CRS:  EPSG:3857  #> # #> # A directed simple graph with 1 component with spatially explicit edges #> # #> # Node data: 16 × 4 (active) #>   osmid     x     y    geometry #>   <int> <dbl> <dbl> <POINT [m]> #> 1     1     0     0       (0 0) #> 2     2     0   100     (0 100) #> 3     3     0   200     (0 200) #> 4     4     0   300     (0 300) #> 5     5   100     0     (100 0) #> 6     6   100   100   (100 100) #> # ℹ 10 more rows #> # #> # Edge data: 48 × 9 #>    from    to     u     v osmid highway  oneway         geometry length #>   <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <chr>    <lgl>  <LINESTRING [m]>  <dbl> #> 1     1     5     1     5     1 residen… FALSE      (0 0, 100 0)    100 #> 2     1     2     1     2     2 residen… FALSE      (0 0, 0 100)    100 #> 3     5     9     5     9     3 residen… FALSE    (100 0, 200 0)    100 #> # ℹ 45 more rows

what: Convert to a tidygraph table graph — ox_as_tidygraph
title: Convert to a tidygraph table graph — ox_as_tidygraph
text: Returns graph tidygraph::tbl_graph(), dropping geometry (node coordinates kept x/y columns).

title: Convert to a tidygraph table graph — ox_as_tidygraph
Convert to a tidygraph table graph — ox_as_tidygraph
text: g osm_graph. directed Build directed graph. Default TRUE.

title: Convert to a tidygraph table graph — ox_as_tidygraph
text: tbl_graph.

title: Convert to a tidygraph table graph — ox_as_tidygraph
code: g <- example_osm_graph() ox_as_tidygraph(g) #> # A tbl_graph: 16 nodes and 48 edges #> # #> # A directed simple graph with 1 component #> # #> # Node Data: 16 × 3 (active) #>    osmid     x     y #>    <int> <dbl> <dbl> #>  1     1     0     0 #>  2     2     0   100 #>  3     3     0   200 #>  4     4     0   300 #>  5     5   100     0 #>  6     6   100   100 #>  7     7   100   200 #>  8     8   100   300 #>  9     9   200     0 #> 10    10   200   100 #> 11    11   200   200 #> 12    12   200   300 #> 13    13   300     0 #> 14    14   300   100 #> 15    15   300   200 #> 16    16   300   300 #> # #> # Edge Data: 48 × 8 #>    from    to     u     v osmid highway     oneway length #>   <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <chr>       <lgl>   <dbl> #> 1     1     5     1     5     1 residential FALSE     100 #> 2     1     2     1     2     2 residential FALSE     100 #> 3     5     9     5     9     3 residential FALSE     100 #> # ℹ 45 more rows

title: Basic street-network statistics — ox_basic_stats
text: Summary measures osm_graph: node edge counts, total mean edge length, mean -degree, self-loop count average circuity. Computation performed bundled Rust core.

title: Basic street-network statistics — ox_basic_stats
Basic street-network statistics — ox_basic_stats
text: g osm_graph. weight Edge column used length/weight. Default "length".

title: Basic street-network statistics — ox_basic_stats
text: one-row tibble statistics.

title: Basic street-network statistics — ox_basic_stats
code: g <- example_osm_graph() ox_basic_stats(g) #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   n_nodes n_edges total_length mean_length mean_out_degree self_loops circuity #>     <int>   <int>        <dbl>       <dbl>           <dbl>      <int>    <dbl> #> 1      16      48         4800         100               3          0        1

title: Compute edge compass bearings — ox_bearings
text: Initial compass bearing (degrees clockwise north) edge, first last coordinate. Geographic coordinates used; projected graphs transformed EPSG:4326 first.

title: Compute edge compass bearings — ox_bearings
Compute edge compass bearings — ox_bearings
text: g osm_graph.

title: Compute edge compass bearings — ox_bearings
text: numeric vector bearings, one per edge.

title: Compute edge compass bearings — ox_bearings
Node centrality — ox_centrality
text: Computes betweenness /closeness centrality every node, using Rust core (Brandes' algorithm betweenness; one Dijkstra per node closeness).

title: Node centrality — ox_centrality
code: ox_centrality(   g,   type = c("betweenness", "closeness"),   weight = "length",   normalized = TRUE )

title: Node centrality — ox_centrality
text: g osm_graph. type Centrality measures compute: "betweenness" "closeness". Default . weight Edge column used weight. Default "length". normalized Scale scores comparability across graphs. Betweenness divided (n - 1)(n - 2); closeness uses Wasserman–Faust correction disconnected graphs. Default TRUE.

title: Node centrality — ox_centrality
text: tibble column osmid plus one column per requested measure.

title: Node centrality — ox_centrality
Average network circuity — ox_circuity
text: ratio total edge length total straight-line (great-circle geographic CRS, Euclidean projected) distance edge endpoints. value 1 means perfectly straight streets; higher values indicate winding networks.

title: Average network circuity — ox_circuity
Average network circuity — ox_circuity
text: g osm_graph.

title: Average network circuity — ox_circuity
text: numeric scalar (>= 1).

title: Average network circuity — ox_circuity
Clear the session cache — ox_clear_cache
text: Empties -memory cache downloaded OpenStreetMap responses.

title: Clear the session cache — ox_clear_cache
Clear the session cache — ox_clear_cache
text: Invisibly NULL.

title: Clear the session cache — ox_clear_cache
Consolidate nearby intersections — ox_consolidate_intersections
text: Merges groups nodes lying within tolerance one another single nodes placed group centroid, rewrites edges consolidated nodes drops resulting self-loops. Useful collapsing multiple OSM nodes represent one complex junction (e.g. dual carriageways).

title: Consolidate nearby intersections — ox_consolidate_intersections
Consolidate nearby intersections — ox_consolidate_intersections
text: g osm_graph. tolerance Distance nodes merged, CRS units. Default 10.

title: Consolidate nearby intersections — ox_consolidate_intersections
text: consolidated osm_graph (meta$consolidated = TRUE).

title: Consolidate nearby intersections — ox_consolidate_intersections
text: Clustering uses sf::st_is_within_distance(); connected components found Rust core. tolerance units graph CRS, project graph first (e.g. metric CRS) meaningful distance.

title: Consolidate nearby intersections — ox_consolidate_intersections
code: g <- example_osm_graph(n = 4, spacing = 100) # nothing is within 10 units here, so the graph is unchanged ox_consolidate_intersections(g, tolerance = 10) #>  #> ── osm_graph ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> 16 nodes, 48 edges #> Network type: "drive" #> Simplified: TRUE #> CRS: "EPSG:3857"

title: Shortest-path distance matrix — ox_distance_matrix
text: Computes matrix minimum-weight distances every node every node (Rust core; one Dijkstra per source).

title: Shortest-path distance matrix — ox_distance_matrix
Shortest-path distance matrix — ox_distance_matrix
text: g osm_graph. Node osmids matrix rows. Node osmids matrix columns. Defaults . weight Edge column used weight. Default "length".

title: Shortest-path distance matrix — ox_distance_matrix
text: numeric matrix (length() x length()) osmid dimnames; Inf marks unreachable pairs.

title: Shortest-path distance matrix — ox_distance_matrix
code: g <- example_osm_graph(n = 3) nodes <- g$nodes$osmid ox_distance_matrix(g, from = nodes[1:2], to = nodes[3:4]) #>     3   4 #> 1 200 100 #> 2 100 200

title: Single-source shortest distances — ox_distances
text: Minimum-weight distance every node graph.

title: Single-source shortest distances — ox_distances
Single-source shortest distances — ox_distances
text: g osm_graph. node osmid. weight Edge column used weight. Default "length".

title: Single-source shortest distances — ox_distances
text: tibble columns osmid distance (Inf unreachable nodes).

title: Single-source shortest distances — ox_distances

what: Download features within a bounding box — ox_features_from_bbox
title: Download features within a bounding box — ox_features_from_bbox
text: Queries OpenStreetMap (via Overpass) elements matching tags — points interest, amenities, buildings, transit stops, — returning sf points (ways relations represented centroid).

title: Download features within a bounding box — ox_features_from_bbox
Download features within a bounding box — ox_features_from_bbox
text: bbox Numeric c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) longitude/latitude. tags Named list OSM tag filters. element either TRUE (key present value) character vector allowed values, e.g. list(amenity = c("school", "hospital")).

title: Download features within a bounding box — ox_features_from_bbox
text: sf POINT features osm_type, osm_id one column per tag encountered.

title: Download features within a bounding box — ox_features_from_bbox
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() bbox <- c(-34.91, -8.07, -34.87, -8.04) ox_features_from_bbox(bbox, tags = list(amenity = "school")) }

Download features for a named place — ox_features_from_place
text: query place name, e.g. "Recife, Brazil". tags Named list OSM tag filters. dist Search half-width metres around geocoded point. Default 2000.

title: Download features for a named place — ox_features_from_place

Compute isochrones (service areas) — ox_isochrone
text: one origin nodes, finds set nodes reachable within cutoff (chosen edge weight — distance , ox_add_edge_travel_times(), travel time) returns polygon per cutoff: hull reachable nodes. several origins, reachability minimum cost origin.

title: Compute isochrones (service areas) — ox_isochrone
Compute isochrones (service areas) — ox_isochrone
text: g osm_graph. center One origin node osmids (see ox_nearest_nodes()). cutoffs Numeric vector cutoff values, units weight. weight Edge column used cost. Default "length". ratio Concavity sf::st_concave_hull() (0 = concave, 1 = convex). Default 0.4.

title: Compute isochrones (service areas) — ox_isochrone
text: sf object one polygon row per cutoff (columns cutoff, n_nodes, geometry), ordered largest smallest cutoff smaller areas draw top.

title: Compute isochrones (service areas) — ox_isochrone
text: Reachable sets come Rust Dijkstra core; hull built sf::st_concave_hull() available (GEOS >= 3.11), falling back convex hull. metric cutoffs, project graph metric CRS first.

title: Compute isochrones (service areas) — ox_isochrone
code: g <- example_osm_graph(n = 6, spacing = 100) center <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, 250, 250) iso <- ox_isochrone(g, center, cutoffs = c(100, 300)) iso #> Simple feature collection with 2 features and 2 fields #> Geometry type: POLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 0 ymin: 0 xmax: 500 ymax: 500 #> Projected CRS: WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator #>   cutoff n_nodes                       geometry #> 1    300      23 POLYGON ((0 200, 0 300, 100... #> 2    100       5 POLYGON ((200 200, 100 200,...

what: k shortest paths between two nodes — ox_k_shortest_paths
title: k shortest paths between two nodes — ox_k_shortest_paths
text: Computes k loopless shortest paths using Yen's algorithm Rust core. Useful route alternatives.

title: k shortest paths between two nodes — ox_k_shortest_paths
k shortest paths between two nodes — ox_k_shortest_paths
text: g osm_graph. , Node osmids. k Number paths return. Default 3. weight Edge column used weight. Default "length".

title: k shortest paths between two nodes — ox_k_shortest_paths
text: tibble one row per path: rank, cost list-column path node osmids, ordered increasing cost. Fewer k rows returned fewer distinct paths exist.

title: k shortest paths between two nodes — ox_k_shortest_paths
code: g <- example_osm_graph() from <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, 0, 0) to <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, 200, 200) ox_k_shortest_paths(g, from, to, k = 3) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>    rank  cost path      #>   <int> <dbl> <list>    #> 1     1   400 <int [5]> #> 2     2   400 <int [5]> #> 3     3   400 <int [5]>

what: Load a graph from GraphML — ox_load_graphml
title: Load a graph from GraphML — ox_load_graphml
text: Reads GraphML file written ox_save_graphml() back osm_graph, restoring node coordinates, edge attributes edge geometry (stored WKT).

title: Load a graph from GraphML — ox_load_graphml
Load a graph from GraphML — ox_load_graphml
text: path Path .graphml file.

title: Load a graph from GraphML — ox_load_graphml
text: osm_graph.

title: Load a graph from GraphML — ox_load_graphml
code: g <- example_osm_graph() f <- tempfile(fileext = ".graphml") ox_save_graphml(g, f) ox_load_graphml(f) #>  #> ── osm_graph ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> 16 nodes, 48 edges #> Network type: "unknown" #> Simplified: FALSE #> CRS: "PROJCRS[\"WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator\", BASEGEOGCRS[\"WGS 84\", #> ENSEMBLE[\"World Geodetic System 1984 ensemble\", MEMBER[\"World Geodetic #> System 1984 (Transit)\"], MEMBER[\"World Geodetic System 1984 (G730)\"], #> MEMBER[\"World Geodetic System 1984 (G873)\"], MEMBER[\"World Geodetic System #> 1984 (G1150)\"], MEMBER[\"World Geodetic System 1984 (G1674)\"], MEMBER[\"World #> Geodetic System 1984 (G1762)\"], MEMBER[\"World Geodetic System 1984 #> (G2139)\"], ELLIPSOID[\"WGS 84\",6378137,298.257223563, #> LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1]], ENSEMBLEACCURACY[2.0]], PRIMEM[\"Greenwich\",0, #> ANGLEUNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433]], ID[\"EPSG\",4326]], #> CONVERSION[\"Popular Visualisation Pseudo-Mercator\", METHOD[\"Popular #> Visualisation Pseudo Mercator\", ID[\"EPSG\",1024]], PARAMETER[\"Latitude of #> natural origin\",0, ANGLEUNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433], #> ID[\"EPSG\",8801]], PARAMETER[\"Longitude of natural origin\",0, #> ANGLEUNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433], ID[\"EPSG\",8802]], PARAMETER[\"False #> easting\",0, LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1], ID[\"EPSG\",8806]], PARAMETER[\"False #> northing\",0, LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1], ID[\"EPSG\",8807]]], CS[Cartesian,2], #> AXIS[\"easting (X)\",east, ORDER[1], LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1]], AXIS[\"northing #> (Y)\",north, ORDER[2], LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1]], USAGE[ SCOPE[\"Web mapping and #> visualisation.\"], AREA[\"World between 85.06°S and 85.06°N.\"], #> BBOX[-85.06,-180,85.06,180]], ID[\"EPSG\",3857]]"

what: Get or set package settings — ox_settings
title: Get or set package settings — ox_settings
text: Configure Overpass Nominatim endpoints, HTTP behaviour caching used ox_* download functions. Called arguments returns current settings list; called named arguments updates returns previous values invisibly.

title: Get or set package settings — ox_settings
Get or set package settings — ox_settings
text: ... Named settings update. Recognised names: overpass_url, nominatim_url, user_agent, timeout, max_tries, cache.

title: Get or set package settings — ox_settings
text: named list settings (current values, previous values invisibly updating).

title: Get or set package settings — ox_settings
code: ox_settings() #> $overpass_url #> [1] "https://overpass-api.de/api/interpreter" #>  #> $nominatim_url #> [1] "https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org" #>  #> $user_agent #> [1] "osmnxr R package (https://github.com/StrategicProjects/osmnxr)" #>  #> $timeout #> [1] 180 #>  #> $max_tries #> [1] 3 #>  #> $cache #> [1] TRUE #>  old <- ox_settings(timeout = 300) ox_settings(timeout = old$timeout) # restore

what: Shortest path between two nodes — ox_shortest_path

title: Shortest path between two nodes — ox_shortest_path
Shortest path between two nodes — ox_shortest_path
text: g osm_graph. , Node osmids (returned ox_nearest_nodes()). weight Edge column used weight. Default "length".

title: Shortest path between two nodes — ox_shortest_path
text: vector node osmids describing path (length 0 target unreachable).

title: Shortest path between two nodes — ox_shortest_path
code: g <- example_osm_graph() from <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, 0, 0) to <- ox_nearest_nodes(g, 300, 300) ox_shortest_path(g, from, to) #> [1]  1  5  9 13 14 15 16

title: Simplify street-network topology — ox_simplify
text: Removes interstitial (degree-2) nodes merely shape geometry street, merging maximal chain nodes single edge whose geometry follows original points whose length sum merged segments. true endpoints intersections kept nodes.

title: Simplify street-network topology — ox_simplify
Simplify street-network topology — ox_simplify
text: g unsimplified osm_graph.

title: Simplify street-network topology — ox_simplify
text: simplified osm_graph (meta$simplified = TRUE).

title: Simplify street-network topology — ox_simplify
text: topology walk performed Rust core; geometry rebuilt sf. Downloaded graphs unsimplified default; standard cleanup step analysis.

title: Simplify street-network topology — ox_simplify
code: g <- example_osm_graph() ox_simplify(g) # already simplified: returned unchanged #> ℹ Graph is already simplified; returning unchanged. #>  #> ── osm_graph ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> 16 nodes, 48 edges #> Network type: "drive" #> Simplified: TRUE #> CRS: "EPSG:3857"

title: Export to GeoJSON — ox_to_geojson

title: Export to GeoJSON — ox_to_geojson
Export to GeoJSON — ox_to_geojson
text: g osm_graph. path Output file path. layer layer write: "edges" (default) "nodes".

title: Export to GeoJSON — ox_to_geojson
text: path, invisibly.

title: Export to GeoJSON — ox_to_geojson

what: Build a MapLibre GL style fragment — ox_to_maplibre
title: Build a MapLibre GL style fragment — ox_to_maplibre
text: Writes edges GeoJSON file returns MapLibre GL JS style fragment (list sources layers) references , ready merge map style. Serialize , e.g., jsonlite::toJSON(..., auto_unbox = TRUE).

title: Build a MapLibre GL style fragment — ox_to_maplibre
code: ox_to_maplibre(   g,   path,   source_id = "osmnxr",   layer_id = "streets",   url = basename(path) )

title: Build a MapLibre GL style fragment — ox_to_maplibre
text: g osm_graph. path GeoJSON output path edge data. source_id, layer_id Identifiers MapLibre source layer. url URL style use fetch data. Defaults basename(path).

title: Build a MapLibre GL style fragment — ox_to_maplibre
text: named list sources layers, invisibly written data path.

title: Build a MapLibre GL style fragment — ox_to_maplibre
code: g <- example_osm_graph() style <- ox_to_maplibre(g, tempfile(fileext = ".geojson")) names(style) #> [1] "sources" "layers"

title: Plot an osm_graph — plot.osm_graph

osmnxr 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: First development version. Initial scaffold OSMnx R. Download street networks OpenStreetMap (ox_graph_from_place(), ox_graph_from_address(), ox_graph_from_point(), ox_graph_from_bbox()) via Overpass API, returning tidy sf-backed osm_graph object. Geocoding Nominatim (ox_geocode(), ox_geocode_to_sf()). Rust compute core (via extendr): CSR graph, Dijkstra routing (ox_shortest_path(), ox_distances(), ox_nearest_nodes()) metrics (ox_basic_stats(), ox_bearings(), ox_orientation_entropy()). example_osm_graph() synthetic grid offline examples tests. Configuration via ox_settings() session caching ox_clear_cache(). Topology cleanup (Rust core): ox_simplify() merges chains interstitial degree-2 nodes single edges; ox_consolidate_intersections() merges nearby nodes one (via connected components); ox_nearest_edges() finds closest edge point. Advanced routing (Rust core): ox_k_shortest_paths() (Yen’s algorithm), ox_distance_matrix() (many--many), ox_isochrone() (reachable-area polygons via multi-source Dijkstra + concave hull). Travel-time routing: ox_add_edge_speeds() ox_add_edge_travel_times() add speed_kph / travel_time edge columns time-weighted shortest paths isochrones. New vignette: “Routing isochrones”. Network metrics (Rust core): ox_centrality() (betweenness via Brandes’ algorithm, closeness) ox_circuity() (street straightness). Circuity now also reported ox_basic_stats(). Interoperability: ox_as_sfnetwork(), ox_as_tidygraph() ox_as_dodgr() hand graph wider R network ecosystem. Export persistence: ox_to_geojson(), ox_to_maplibre() (style fragment), ox_save_graphml() / ox_load_graphml() lossless round-trip (edge geometry stored WKT), compatible OSMnx / NetworkX / Gephi. Feature downloads: ox_features_from_place() ox_features_from_bbox() fetch POIs, buildings amenities OpenStreetMap tidy sf points. ox_plot_orientation() draws polar rose plot street bearings (ggplot2). Documentation: vignettes “Urban metrics”, “Street orientation”, “Features points interest”, “Interoperability export” “Accessibility analysis”; full pkgdown reference articles menu.
